content: Herd immunity occurs when sufficient population immunity reduces disease transmission, indirectly protecting susceptible individuals. The herd immunity threshold (HIT) is calculated as (1 - 1/R0), representing the proportion needing immunity to achieve Rt < 1. For measles (R0≈15), HIT is ~93%; for polio (R0≈6), HIT is ~83%. HIT varies by disease transmissibility, population mixing patterns, and vaccine effectiveness. Natural infection and vaccination both contribute to herd immunity, but vaccination achieves it without disease burden. Heterogeneous mixing means HIT may be lower if high-transmission groups are prioritized.
source: WHO vaccination guidelines, CDC epidemiology principles

content: Achieving herd immunity requires sustained high vaccination coverage. Strategies include: routine immunization programs, catch-up campaigns, school entry requirements, and targeting high-transmission groups. Vaccine effectiveness modifies the required coverage: if vaccine efficacy is 90%, coverage must exceed HIT/0.9. Waning immunity necessitates boosters. Herd immunity thresholds assume homogeneous mixing; in reality, geographic pockets of low coverage enable outbreaks even with high national coverage. Elimination requires maintaining immunity above HIT despite importation risk. Herd immunity cannot be achieved for diseases without lasting immunity or animal reservoirs.
source: CDC Pink Book, WHO position papers on vaccines

content: HIT calculation refinements account for: pre-existing cross-immunity, age-structured contact patterns, spatial heterogeneity, and waning immunity. Basic formula (1 - 1/R0) assumes perfect, lifelong immunity and random mixing—rarely true. Age-stratified models weight immunity by contact rates (children have higher contacts for many diseases). Seroprevalence surveys measure actual population immunity. For SARS-CoV-2, evolving variants and waning immunity made stable herd immunity unlikely. Herd immunity should be viewed as a dynamic equilibrium requiring ongoing vaccination, not a one-time achievement.
